field_reference.top_level_array: Each element is one leak detector (A2D stack) on the platform.

detector_object.Name: string, required. Human-readable detector id.
detector_object.NumChnl: number, required. Channel count for this detector (1-based channel indices under leakage/<i>/).
detector_object.ChnlNames: array of strings, optional. If set, written as channel_name file inside leakage/<i>/<j>/.
detector_object.Device: array of device entries. Default BOM-alternative mode: mutually-exclusive populations, first probed I2C match drives all NumChnl channels. Per-channel device-map mode (auto-selected when entry count == NumChnl and all Bus/Address pairs are distinct): entry k is the sensor for logical channel k, each configured and linked independently.

device_object.DeviceType: string, required. e.g. MAX1363, ADS1015, ADS7924 — maps to driver / register logic.
device_object.Address: string, required. Hex address; alternatives may share one address when BOMs are mutually exclusive (list MAX1363 before ADS1015).
device_object.ChannelId: number, optional. Hardware A2D channel (1-based) wired on this device; defaults to the logical channel index (1..NumChnl). Used for IIO sysfs links (in_voltage<N>_raw) and ADS1015 MUX/threshold programming when channels are not sequential.
device_object.Probe: boolean, optional. If true, bind kernel driver (new_device) before register writes.
device_object.Type: string, optional. Sensor kind label (e.g. rop, flex). Published as runtime .../<j>/type.
device_object.CfgRegVal: string, optional. Space-separated byte values for CfgReg. ADS1015: first byte is config high (MUX for ChannelId — 0xc2/0xd2/0xe2/0xf2 for channels 1–4), second byte is config low (mode/comparator). MAX1363: platform monitor burst; byte0 = scan-to-CS (0x01/0x03/0x05/0x07 for channels 1–4), first 0x1x byte = monitor channel tag (0x11/0x13/0x15/0x1f). Script patches these from ChannelId when needed.
device_object.LoThreshReg: string, optional. Low threshold register address.
device_object.LoThreshRegVal: string, optional. Bytes for low threshold (big-endian); scaled to min.
device_object.HiThreshReg: string, optional. High threshold register address.
device_object.HiThreshRegVal: string, optional. Bytes for high threshold; scaled to max.
device_object.NormalMin: number, optional. Direct runtime .../<j>/min when LoThreshRegVal is absent.
device_object.NormalMax: number, optional. Direct runtime .../<j>/max when HiThreshRegVal is absent.
device_object.Scale: number, optional. Multiplier from raw/counts to engineering units (min/max).
device_object.WarningMin: number, optional. User low-side warning bound (engineering units). Runtime: .../<j>/lwarn; also fills min if NormalMin absent.
device_object.CriticalMin: number, optional. User low-side critical bound (engineering units). Runtime: .../<j>/lcrit.
device_object.WarningMax: number, optional. User high-side warning bound. Runtime: .../<j>/warn; also fills max if NormalMax absent.
device_object.CriticalMax: number, optional. User high-side critical bound. Runtime: .../<j>/crit.
device_object.Ads7924SoftReset: boolean, optional. Default true: write 0xaa to RESET before programming (raw I2C only).
device_object.Ads7924IntConfig: string, optional. Hex byte INTCONFIG (0x12), default 0xe0.
device_object.Ads7924SlpConfig: string, optional. Hex byte SLPCONFIG (0x13), default 0x00.
device_object.Ads7924AcqConfig: string, optional. Hex byte ACQCONFIG (0x14), default 0x00.
device_object.Ads7924PwrConfig: string, optional. Hex byte PWRCONFIG (0x15), default 0x00.
device_object.Ads7924Mode: string, optional. MODECNTRL final mode byte (reg 0x00), default 0x33 (auto-scan).
device_object.Ads7924AwakeMode: string, optional. MODECNTRL awake step before Ads7924Mode, default 0x20.
device_object.Ads7924UlChN: string, optional. Per-channel ULR override, N=1..4, hex byte.
device_object.Ads7924LlChN: string, optional. Per-channel LLR override, N=1..4, hex byte.
device_object.Ads7924_configuration_note: All Ads7924* keys are optional. When omitted, hw-management-bmc-a2d-leakage-config.sh uses defaults: soft reset on (0xaa @ RESET), INT/SLP/ACQ/PWR = 0xe0 0x00 0x00 0x00, INTCNTRL AEN = 0x0f, AWAKE 0x20 then MODE 0x33 (auto-scan). For ULR/LLR and comparator-driven INT you need Scale plus NormalMin/NormalMax (or if those are absent, WarningMin/WarningMax) on raw I2C (Probe false). Per-channel ULR/LLR overrides use Ads7924UlCh1..4 / Ads7924LlCh1..4 only when you need registers to differ from that voltage-derived window.

runtime_paths.leakage_i_j: /var/run/hw-management/leakage/<i>/<j>/ where i = detector index, j = channel. j is 1..NumChnl (BOM-alternative mode) or the 1-based Device[] position (per-channel device-map mode; may be non-contiguous when an earlier device is absent).
runtime_paths.input: symlink to the kernel IIO raw attribute for the wired hardware channel: in_voltage<ChannelId-1>_raw (defaults to in_voltage<j-1>_raw when ChannelId is absent).

field_reference.deployment_note: Copy only the array in example_leak_detectors to /etc/hw-management-bmc-a2d-leakage-config.json (or merge with platform file). The full JSON in this file is for documentation; the BMC tool expects a top-level JSON array.

example_leak_detectors.Name: Example_PerChannel_DeviceMap_NonSequential
example_leak_detectors._comment: Per-channel device-map mode: 2 distinct devices == NumChnl, so Device[0] -> channel 1, Device[1] -> channel 2. Each sensor is wired to hardware AIN3 (ChannelId 4), so input links to in_voltage3_raw and the ADS1015 MUX byte is 0xf2.
